summary: Unified Flow Fix VERIFIED: All 7 action endpoints correctly return unified flow IDs (ticket_id, notification_id, inbox_id). Backend tests 100% passing (9/9). Timestamp format is consistent for NEW entries. ISSUE FOUND: Mixed timestamp types in database (datetime vs string) causing sorting inconsistency in unified inbox.

minor.issue: Mixed timestamp types in database - older entries stored as datetime objects, newer entries as strings. This causes MongoDB to sort datetime entries before string entries, making new entries appear below old ones.
minor.root_cause: Historical data was stored with datetime objects, new data uses ISO string format from get_utc_timestamp()
minor.suggested_fix: Run data migration to convert all datetime timestamps to ISO string format

BACKEND_TEST_09_db_verification.details: Care request creates entries in admin_notifications, service_desk_tickets, channel_intakes

TIMESTAMP_TEST_01_notifications_sorted.details: New notification appears at TOP of admin notifications list

TIMESTAMP_TEST_02_unified_inbox_sorted.details: New inbox entry appears at position 26 instead of TOP due to mixed timestamp types in database
TIMESTAMP_TEST_02_unified_inbox_sorted.root_cause: 26 entries have datetime type, 229 have string type. MongoDB sorts datetime before strings in descending order.

TIMESTAMP_TEST_03_format_consistency.details: New timestamps use correct format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.fff+00:00

FRONTEND_TEST_01_homepage_mobile.details: Homepage loads correctly on mobile viewport (375x667)

FRONTEND_TEST_02_care_page_mobile.details: Care page loads on mobile. 'Get Care Now' button visible.

FRONTEND_TEST_03_travel_page_mobile.details: Travel page loads on mobile. 'Plan My Trip' button visible.

FRONTEND_TEST_04_admin_dashboard_mobile.details: Admin dashboard loads on mobile after login. Command Center, Service Desk, Unified Inbox visible.

action_items: DATA MIGRATION REQUIRED: Convert all datetime timestamps to ISO string format in admin_notifications, channel_intakes, and service_desk_tickets collections
critical_code_review_comments: timestamp_utils.py correctly implements get_utc_timestamp() returning YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.fff+00:00 format
All pillar routes (travel, care, fit, enjoy, dine) correctly use get_utc_timestamp() for new entries
UnifiedFlowMiddleware in unified_flow_enforcer.py correctly monitors action endpoints
ISSUE: Historical data has datetime objects instead of strings, causing MongoDB sorting issues

success_rate.timestamp_sorting: 66% (2/3 - unified inbox sorting fails due to data migration needed)

seed_data_creation: Test requests created with TEST_* prefixed pet names for easy identification
context_for_next_testing_agent: Unified flow fix is working correctly for NEW entries. All 7 action endpoints return ticket_id, notification_id, inbox_id. The timestamp format is consistent (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.fff+00:00). HOWEVER, there's a data migration needed: 26-27 old entries in each collection have datetime type instead of string type, causing MongoDB to sort them before string entries. Main agent should run a migration to convert all datetime timestamps to ISO strings.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: New entries not appearing at TOP of unified inbox list
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Mixed timestamp types in MongoDB - older entries stored as datetime objects, newer entries as ISO strings. MongoDB sorts datetime type before string type in descending order.
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Run data migration to convert all datetime timestamps to ISO string format
